---
title: "Course"
toc: false
---

::: {.offering-banner}
<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED OFFERING BANNER -->
**Fall 2026**

- **Dates:** 2026-10-20 – 2027-01-26
- **Meeting:** Tuesdays 10:00–12:00
- **Instructor(s):** [Course staff](https://github.com/applied-dp-course)
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED OFFERING BANNER -->
:::

Applied Differential Privacy is a hands-on course on differential privacy and
privacy-preserving machine learning. Lectures pair narrative slides with self-study notebooks;
computation is imported from the course library
[`libdpy`](https://github.com/applied-dp-course/pub_lib).

## Prerequisites

Familiarity with undergraduate probability, basic linear algebra, and Python. Prior exposure to
machine learning is helpful but not required.

## Grading and policies

Grading breakdown, collaboration rules, and late-work policies are set by the instructor each term
and announced on the course forum.

## Logistics (current offering)

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED SYLLABUS LOGISTICS -->
### Fall 2026

- **Dates:** 2026-10-20 – 2027-01-26
- **Meeting:** Tuesdays 10:00–12:00
- **Instructor(s):** [Course staff](https://github.com/applied-dp-course)
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED SYLLABUS LOGISTICS -->

## Course pages

- **[Syllabus](syllabus.qmd)** — weekly plan with links to lectures, notebooks, and assignments
- **[Lectures](lectures.qmd)** — self-study notebooks and slide decks for each topic
- **[Class assignments](class-assignments.qmd)** — in-class interactive problem sets
- **[Home assignments](home-assignments.qmd)** — take-home problem sets
- **[Past years](archive.qmd)** — schedules from previous offerings
